articles.title: A Rare Offering of Scale: 325,000 RSF at 1285 Avenue of the Americas
articles.narrative: RXR is marketing a rare, large-scale office leasing opportunity at 1285 Avenue of the Americas in Midtown Manhattan. The offering comprises approximately 325,000 rentable square feet of contiguous space across five floors, providing a unique chance for global firms to establish a significant corporate presence in a prime business corridor. The building, designed by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, offers expansive and flexible floor plates, high ceilings, and direct connectivity to Rockefeller Center, catering to the evolving needs of large tenants seeking a dominant headquarters in a prestigious location with extensive transit access.

data_points.address: 1285 Avenue of the Americas, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located on Corporate Row between 51st and 52nd streets, offering concourse-level connectivity to Rockefeller Center and proximity to major transit hubs.

articles.title: Sunday Summary: Gary Barnett Has Only Just Begun!
articles.narrative: Extell Development has acquired a significant development site at 405-417 Park Avenue in New York City for $500 million, with plans to potentially expand by acquiring two adjacent buildings. The developer is considering a project that could yield up to 700,000 square feet of rentable office space with air rights, or 527,000 square feet of as-of-right development. This acquisition underscores continued confidence in prime Manhattan development opportunities.

data_points.address: 405-417 Park Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Block-long development site between East 54th and East 55th streets.
data_points.project_notes: Developer is also pursuing two adjacent buildings: 110 East 55th Street and potentially 111 East 54th Street.

articles.title: Baltimore Industrial Recalibration: Driven by New Supply and Selective Leasing
articles.narrative: Baltimore's industrial market is undergoing a recalibration rather than a contraction, characterized by rising vacancy and elevated supply, yet sustained demand for high-quality assets. The market is exhibiting a 'barbell dynamic,' with strong performance in bulk logistics facilities in premier submarkets like Baltimore County East and smaller infill properties under 100,000 square feet. This divergence indicates that demand is becoming more selective, with capital increasingly disciplined and performance tied to asset quality and strategic positioning within the region's evolving logistics network. Despite broader market headwinds, institutional capital continues to invest, as evidenced by a significant portfolio acquisition, underscoring confidence in Baltimore's long-term strategic advantages as an East Coast logistics hub.
articles.market_intelligence: CoStar Group characterizes the Baltimore industrial market as undergoing a 'sharp correction' due to rising vacancy, elevated supply, and slower leasing activity. The market's vacancy rate is approximately 9.7%, with 3.2 million square feet of new deliveries over the past 12 months and negative trailing absorption of 2.4 million square feet. The new development pipeline stands at 2.1 million square feet. Baltimore County East leads the region with approximately 660,000 square feet of positive absorption over the trailing 12 months, holds 16.2% of market inventory, and has the most active construction pipeline at 664,000 square feet. Industrial transaction volume is holding near $1.1 billion, with pricing averaging approximately $142 per square foot and cap rates around 7.7%.

articles.title: Triten Real Estate Partners completes 400,000-square-foot distribution center in Humble
articles.narrative: Triten Real Estate Partners has completed Kenswick at 1960, a two-building, Class-A distribution center totaling nearly 400,000 square feet in Humble, Texas. The development is strategically located near George Bush Intercontinental Airport and major freight corridors, offering efficient access to the Houston metropolitan area and beyond. The project features a campus-style layout, 36-foot clear heights, extensive trailer parking, and the potential for over five acres of secured outdoor storage, catering to a diverse range of industrial users.

data_points.notable_features: Dual-building campus-style environment with 36-foot clear heights, ample dock-door count, approximately 9,000 square feet of Class A office space, and above-standard trailer parking with potential for over five acres of secured outdoor storage. Located approximately one-half mile from George Bush Intercontinental Airport's air cargo facilities, with immediate connectivity to U.S. Highway 59 (Interstate 69), Beltway 8, and Grand Parkway (SH 99).

articles.title: All that new supply? It’s suppressing apartment rent growth
articles.narrative: A new report indicates that U.S. multifamily rent growth remains sluggish due to a historic wave of new supply, particularly in Sun Belt and Mountain West markets. While national average advertised rents saw a modest $4 increase in April to $1,758, year-over-year growth was negative 0.2%, and year-to-date growth was only 0.4%. Gateway and Midwest markets, including New York City, San Francisco, Chicago, and Minneapolis-St. Paul, are outperforming, with the Twin Cities projected for strong year-end growth due to more restrained new supply. Despite economic headwinds and moderating demand, opportunities may exist for investors in distressed assets and for operators focusing on expense reductions.
articles.market_intelligence: National multifamily advertised rents increased by $4 in April 2026 to $1,758, marking the second consecutive month of growth after winter declines. However, national rents were down 0.2% year-over-year and up only 0.4% year-to-date through April, which is about one-third of the average growth pace seen between 2012 and 2019. High-supply markets like Austin (-4.3%), Denver (-3.6%), Tampa (-3.4%), and Phoenix (-2.7%) continued to show weak annual rent growth. In contrast, Gateway and Midwest markets like New York City (4.8%), San Francisco (4.1%), Chicago (3.3%), and Minneapolis-St. Paul (2.4%) outperformed. The national multifamily occupancy rate stood at 94.2% in March, down 0.5% year-over-year. Nearly 480,000 rental units are forecast to be delivered nationally in 2026, followed by about 450,000 units annually thereafter.

articles.title: Marcus & Millichap closes sale of 36-unit apartment property in Falcon Heights
articles.narrative: Marcus & Millichap has brokered the sale of Falcon View Apartments, a 36-unit multifamily property in Falcon Heights, Minnesota, which traded for $5.09 million. The property, consisting of two buildings, offers a mix of one-, two-, and three-bedroom floor plans.

data_points.address: 1800-1818 Larpenteur Ave. W., Falcon Heights, MN
data_points.notable_features: Located near the University of Minnesota's St. Paul campus.

articles.title: Texas Icon Ford Noe: Resetting brokerage growth through systems and talent development
articles.narrative: This article profiles Ford Noe, the Senior Managing Director and Market Leader of Marcus & Millichap's Houston office, highlighting his strategic leadership in transforming the office into one of the firm's top-performing markets. Since taking the helm in 2018, Noe has quadrupled headcount and revenue, overseeing over $10 billion in transactional volume. His success is attributed to a focus on talent development and scalable systems, including the creation of a standardized, firmwide Summer Internship Program that has become a competitive entry point for aspiring commercial real estate professionals.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: Southern Methodist University's Cox School of Business

people.name: Ford Noe
people.title: Senior Managing Director and Market Leader

articles.title: What you missed at the NYC Real Estate Forum
articles.narrative: This article summarizes key discussions and debates from The Real Deal's NYC Real Estate Forum, highlighting various perspectives on the city's real estate market and policy. Topics included a defense of New York City's real estate market and criticism of tax programs, a contentious debate on brokerage listing transparency, and strong opposition to a proposed pied-à-terre tax. Additionally, discussions covered Vornado's commitment to a major office development, the Housing Preservation and Development Commissioner's efforts to improve landlord relations, and insights into a casino project's strategic site selection. The forum also featured a panel on the private credit market and a discussion on luxury development trends in Brooklyn and Queens.

articles.title: South Florida’s top deals: Waterfront Venetian Islands property trades for $35M
articles.narrative: An affiliate of Alta Development acquired the Roadway Inn South Miami, a four-story, 117-key hotel property spanning approximately 96,000 square feet, for $23 million. The seller was an LLC tied to Francisco Martinez-Miyashiki of Pegaso. This transaction highlights continued investor interest in the South Florida hospitality market.

articles.title: Despite Lincoln Park retail boom, LLJ working through $10M loan default
articles.narrative: LLJ Ventures, a San Diego-based property investment firm, defaulted on a nearly $10 million loan against The Armitage Collection, a 33,000-square-foot retail property in Chicago's Lincoln Park. The loan, originated in late 2015, matured on January 6, leading special servicer Midland Loan Services to prepare for foreclosure and receivership. However, Midland is also considering a loan modification, and LLJ Ventures is reportedly close to an agreement to avoid formal foreclosure. The property is currently being marketed for sale by Mid-America Real Estate for $10.5 million, despite a recent appraisal valuing it at $13.1 million.

data_points.notable_features: Anchored by a childcare center with a 10-year renewal, and includes trendy lifestyle tenants. Located near the $3 billion Foundry Park riverfront megaproject.
data_points.project_notes: Loan taken out in late 2015, matured on January 6. Property appraised at $13.1 million in March.

articles.market_intelligence: Lincoln Park's Armitage Avenue is experiencing a retail boom with low vacancy rates, attracting boutiques and digitally native brands. The area is seeing significant development, including the $3 billion Foundry Park riverfront megaproject, which plans 3,200 new housing units, primarily apartments.

articles.title: Michael Shvo, Germans’ exit from Transamerica Pyramid gets more complicated
articles.narrative: The sale of San Francisco's iconic Transamerica Pyramid has become embroiled in a legal dispute, with a new court filing raising questions about the true compensation received by asset manager Michael Shvo. The property, along with two adjacent buildings, was sold by a partnership including German institutional investors and Shvo to Cyprus-based Yoda PLC for $692 million. While Shvo initially reported receiving $34 million in fees, a lawsuit by Deutsche Finance America alleges he received an additional $45 million upon termination as asset manager, bringing his total to $79 million, while Deutsche Finance America claims it was not paid its owed $31 million. This controversy unfolds as the investors incurred significant losses, having acquired the property for $650 million in 2020 and invested $250 million in renovations.

data_points.project_notes: The property was previously acquired for $650 million in 2020, with an additional $250 million invested in renovations.

articles.title: Data Centers Are Becoming Industrial’s Growth Engine
articles.narrative: The artificial intelligence boom is significantly boosting the U.S. industrial real estate sector, extending beyond just data center development to manufacturing and logistics. Major tech and manufacturing firms are partnering to build new facilities, with Nvidia and Corning planning three manufacturing plants in North Carolina and Texas. This trend is validated by a 28% jump in manufacturing leasing activity in Q1 2026 compared to the previous year, with 27% of new manufacturing leases attributed to data center and tech supply chain companies. The demand is driven by the ongoing need for IT gear and critical systems, suggesting a stabilized long-term growth engine for industrial real estate rather than a temporary construction-related surge. Additionally, a growing network of logistics facilities is emerging to support the data center sector, with data center suppliers now accounting for 10% of new industrial leases for major logistics landlords like Prologis.

people.name: Chris Caton
people.title: Managing Director, Global Strategy and Analytics
